name: AttributeDictionaries
summary: The AttributeDictionaries class is a collection of all of the AttributeDictionary objects that are attached to a given Entity object. The Entity class is a popular parent class in SketchUp, meaning you can attach AttributeDictionaries to almost anything, from geometric items like edges and faces and components to more conceptual things like pages or materials. You access this class not by performing an AttributeDictionaries.new but by grabbing a handle from an existing entity.

methods.remarks: Get an AttributeDictionary by name. Returns nil if there is none with the given name.

params.name: key
params.doc: The name of the attribute dictionary.

methods.name: count
methods.summary: The count method is inherited from the Enumerable mix-in module.
methods.remarks: The count method is inherited from the Enumerable mix-in module.

methods.name: delete
methods.summary: The delete method destroys a given AttributeDictionary.
methods.remarks: The delete method destroys a given AttributeDictionary. This AttributeDictionary can be passed directly or identified by its string name. In SketchUp 2018, special attribute dictionaries have been added. The name of these dictionaries are “SU_InstanceSet” and “SU_DefinitionSet”. The dictionaries cannot be deleted via ruby and an ArgumentError will be raised. The key/value pairs in the dictionary can be deleted safely. object

params.name: key_or_dict
params.doc: The name of the attribute dictionary to delete, or the dictionary object itself.

methods.name: each
methods.summary: The #each method is used to iterate through all of the attributes dictionaries.
methods.remarks: The #each method is used to iterate through all of the attributes dictionaries.

methods.name: length
methods.summary: The #length method returns the number of attribute dictionary objects in the collection.
methods.remarks: The #length method returns the number of attribute dictionary objects in the collection.

returns.doc: the number of attribute dictionary objects in the collection.

methods.name: size
methods.summary: The #size method is an alias of #length.
methods.remarks: The #size method is an alias of #length.
